items.title: Run Bugbot before you push
items.why: Move review left so the same agent catches bugs on the diff before the PR roundtrip starts.
how_to_steps: Connect the repository to Cursor and enable Bugbot for the repo in the dashboard.
Run /review or /review-bugbot locally before pushing so the agent inspects the exact diff first.
Turn on incremental review or mention-only mode to keep feedback focused on new commits.
Require the Cursor Bugbot check in branch protection once the team trusts the signal.
items.evidence: Cursor says Bugbot is over 3x faster, 22% cheaper, finds 10% more bugs per review, and 90% of runs now finish in under 3 minutes.
items.try_monday: Enable Bugbot on one active repo and require its check on draft PRs for one week.

items.title: Index the repo so agents can search by meaning, not just strings
items.why: Semantic retrieval cuts the time an agent spends hunting for context and reduces follow-up correction loops.
how_to_steps: Open the workspace and let indexing begin automatically; semantic search becomes available at about 80% completion.
Check indexing status or trigger a rebuild from Cursor Settings > Indexing.
Add large generated paths to .cursorignore or .gitignore so the index stays focused on useful code.
Prompt the agent with behaviors like "where do we handle failed payments?" and let it combine semantic search with grep.
items.evidence: Cursor reports 12.5% higher codebase QA accuracy on average, 0.3% higher code retention overall, 2.6% higher retention on repos with 1,000+ files, and 2.2% more dissatisfied follow-ups when semantic search is unavailable.
items.try_monday: Re-index the main repo, trim noisy paths with .cursorignore, and compare agent answer quality on one thorny codebase question set.
